description_long: This object is a detailed model of the Roman Colosseum, an iconic ancient amphitheater characterized by its massive elliptical structure and multiple tiers of arched openings. The exterior features three levels of arcades, each adorned with rows of evenly spaced arches and columns, giving the facade a rhythmic and monumental appearance. The interior reveals concentric seating tiers surrounding a central arena, with partial wall sections missing to expose the inner structure. The stonework is weathered, with a palette of light beige and gray tones, and the overall form is partially ruined, reflecting centuries of historical wear. The open central area and the broken outer walls allow a clear view of the architectural complexity and the layered construction. The model captures the grandeur and scale of this ancient Roman landmark, making it instantly recognizable as a historical attraction.
description: A partially ruined, multi-tiered stone amphitheater with arched facades and an open central arena, modeled after the Roman Colosseum. The structure features weathered stonework and exposed interior seating.
description_view_0: This view highlights the broken section of the Colosseum, revealing the interior seating tiers and the elliptical arena.
description_view_1: The front view displays the grand arched facade with three visible levels and the main entrance area, emphasizing the monumentality of the structure.
description_view_2: This angle shows the opposite broken side, exposing the inner walls and the layered seating arrangement.
description_view_3: The rear view presents the continuous arcades and the curvature of the amphitheater, with less visible interior detail.
